requirements.requirement: three randomized repeats per condition and 18 valid Sol-low arms
requirements.evidence: protocol-v8-paper-loop.json records nine pairs, 18 valid arms, opposite randomized judge aliases, and Sol-low execution
one baseline instruction SHA-256 is shared by all valid arms
one frozen portable-runtime SHA-256 is shared by treatment arms
Anthropic response metadata and OpenAI session metadata identify the two v9 models; packets hide condition and use opposite aliases
v9 has four disagreements but only one v9-rule-compliant adjudication; three older outcomes used a different rule
raw pair-preference agreement was 4/9 in v8 and 5/9 in v9

requirements.requirement: confidence intervals for quality, correctness, time, token, and cost effects
requirements.evidence: protocol-v8-paper-loop.json records fixed-seed paired-bootstrap intervals and an input commitment
v9 records one missing-adjudication deviation; v10 records a call-cap overrun and an unplanned no-output failure
public receipts retain generic aggregates and commitments; the project-term/path leak scan returns zero matches

requirements.requirement: total spend at or below 10 USD
requirements.evidence: accounted total is 8.7374338 USD; actual Codex billing is unavailable

why_same-answer_rerun_is_not_completion: Judge instructions were calibrated after observing disagreements on these answers. Selecting another judge pair on the same answers would be post-outcome model selection, not held-out confirmation.
why_fresh_confirmation_cannot_fit_curren: The remaining accounted budget cannot safely fund a new 18-arm held-out evaluation plus two judges and required adjudication.
required_external_change: Start a new budgeted study frame with held-out cases and immutable external preregistration, or explicitly increase the current 10 USD ceiling. Existing failed rounds must remain reported.
